headline: Codex Security enters research preview, promising advanced AI security analysis.

items.title: Codex Security: now in research preview
items.summary: Codex Security, OpenAI's latest AI application security agent, is now available in research preview. It leverages AI to analyze project context for detecting, validating, and patching complex vulnerabilities. This tool aims to enhance security by reducing noise and increasing confidence in vulnerability detection. It represents an innovative approach to application security by integrating AI to manage code security challenges more effectively.
items.why_it_matters: Tech leads should look into Codex Security for advanced AI-driven vulnerability management.

items.title: How Descript enables multilingual video dubbing at scale
items.summary: Descript has integrated OpenAI models to facilitate multilingual video dubbing, enhancing the translation process for both meaning and timing. This ensures that dubbed speech sounds natural in multiple languages, thereby improving the accessibility and reach of video content globally.

items.title: QwenLM/Qwen-Agent
items.summary: Qwen-Agent is a new framework built on Qwen>=3.0, featuring capabilities such as Function Calling, MCP, Code Interpreter, RAG, and Chrome extensions. It is designed to enhance the functionality and integration of agents in various applications.

items.title: How to scan for vulnerabilities with GitHub Security Lab’s open source AI-powered framework
items.summary: GitHub Security Lab has released an AI-powered framework capable of identifying high-impact vulnerabilities such as Auth Bypasses, IDORs, and Token Leaks. This open-source framework aims to enhance software security by leveraging AI to automate and improve the vulnerability scanning process.

all_items.title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
all_items.summary: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct mass surveillance on Americans?
